negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing the exact deadline invariant: default is 300ms instead of 500ms, scheduled obligation scanning is not deadline-threaded, and deterministic obligation caps required by the invariant are absent.
related_but_insufficient_code: The payload builder does establish global_deadline and checks it before sequencing-rights reordering and transaction execution.
Block verification appears not to use a verifier-specific timeout, but the associated deterministic cap/default-500/deadline-threading requirements are incomplete.

rationale: This collapses start-of-build deadline creation, the current 500ms default, deadline-aware scheduled scanning and execution, sequencing-rights simulation, post-revival gating, and the repeated removal of verifier-only deadlines.
statement: Payload building must establish one global deadline at the beginning of default_ethereum_payload using RETH_BUILD_DEADLINE_MS with a 500ms default, thread that deadline through scheduled obligation scanning, transaction execution, sequencing-rights reordering, and the post-checkAndRevive phase boundary, while block verification must compute scheduled obligations without a verifier-specific timeout and rely on deterministic obligation caps for boundedness.
suggested_falsification_tests: With RETH_BUILD_DEADLINE_MS unset, assert EthereumBuilderConfig::new().build_deadline == 500ms; current code returns 300ms.
Create an expensive scheduled obligation scan and assert payload build deadline is honored during scanning and post-checkAndRevive boundary.
Create more obligations than the deterministic cap and assert builder/verifier membership remains symmetric.
summary: Payload has a global deadline, but the invariant is not fully satisfied.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Unset RETH_BUILD_DEADLINE_MS and assert the required 500ms default; current port has DEFAULT_BUILD_DEADLINE_MS = 300.
title: Payload and verifier deadlines avoid asymmetric obligation membership
